cit.basis: Impôt sur les Sociétés (IS). Finance Law 2023 four-year convergence (2023-2026) to a two-rate target system: 20% for net taxable profit below MAD 100,000,000 and 35% for MAD 100,000,000 or more; credit institutions and insurance/reinsurance companies converge to 40%. The applicable rate applies to the WHOLE taxable profit once the threshold is crossed (no marginal slices in the target system).
cit.convergence_note: 2025 is year 3 of the Finance Law 2023 glide path; target rates are fully effective from 2026. This estimator uses the 2026 TARGET rates as the simplest defensible model — the exact 2025 transitional rates differ slightly per bracket (year-3 of the glide path).

cotisation_minimale.note: Minimum contribution (cotisation minimale) of 0.25% of turnover is generally due even in loss-making years; out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: TVA standard rate 20%, with reduced rates of 14%, 10% and 7% for specified goods and services.

social_solidarity_contribution.note: A social solidarity contribution (contribution sociale de solidarité) on profits above statutory thresholds has applied in recent finance laws; check the current finance law for applicability and rates. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

social_insurance.note: CNSS employer and employee contributions apply to payroll; not modelled in this SME estimator.

official_sources.name: Direction Générale des Impôts (DGI), Kingdom of Morocco

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; 2025 is year 3 of the 2023-2026 rate convergence, so statutory transitional rates for 2025 differ slightly from the 2026 target rates used here.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
2026 target rates are used; exact 2025 transitional (year-3 glide path) rates differ slightly per bracket.
Cotisation minimale, social solidarity contribution, WHT, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official DGI guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
